title: Burden of disease
subtitle: [Disability-Adjusted Life Years](#dod:dalys) (DALYs) per 100,000 individuals from all causes. DALYs measure the total burden of disease – both from years of life lost due to premature death and years lived with a disability. One DALY equals one lost year of healthy life.
caption:
Burden of disease

Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs) per 100,000 individuals from all causes. DALYs measure the total burden of disease – both from years of life lost due to premature death and years lived with a disability. One DALY equals one lost year of healthy life.

This choropleth map displays the global distribution of health loss in 2021, quantified as Disability-Adjusted Life Years per 100,000 individuals. The visualization employs a continuous color scale ranging from light beige, representing 0 DALYs, to deep maroon, indicating values exceeding 60,000 DALYs. The highest concentrations of disease burden appear across sub-Saharan Africa, where nations including the Central African Republic, Chad, Lesotho, and Somalia fall within the darkest maroon band, signifying rates above 60,000 per 100,000 people. Surrounding these areas, countries such as Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Mozambique occupy the adjacent red band, corresponding to values between 50,000 and 60,000. In contrast, the lowest burdens are visible across Western Europe, North America, and Australia, where nations like Norway, Canada, and the United States sit in the lightest beige and pale orange bands, generally falling below 20,000 DALYs. Russia presents a distinct anomaly in the northern hemisphere, shaded in dark red to indicate a burden between 50,000 and 60,000, which stands significantly higher than its European neighbors. South America and parts of Southeast Asia display intermediate values, with Brazil and Indonesia appearing in the orange shades that denote ranges between 20,000 and 40,000. Greenland and parts of the Sahara are marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no available data. The geographic pattern shows a clear gradient where the highest disease burden clusters in central and southern Africa, while the lowest burden clusters in the high-income regions of the northern hemisphere and Oceania.
